issue_title: feat: add Salesforce (sfdc) freshness prober via the sf CLI
issue_body:
## Context

The batch-by-system freshness probe engine in #3 ships probers for Slack, Gmail, Aha, and Drive. It deliberately scopes Salesforce, Jira, and GitHub as `no probe available`, so their links render `unchecked` rather than carrying a freshness verdict. Tasks routinely link Salesforce Cases and Opportunities, so those links stay dark on every run. Promoting Salesforce to a real prober closes that gap.

Salesforce access here goes through the `sf` CLI (formerly `sfdx`), which already holds the org auth. The prober should reuse that CLI rather than build a direct HTTP or OAuth client, which keeps the auth story out of the app entirely.

## Goal

Add `internal/probe/salesforce`, a `probe.Prober` that answers Salesforce link freshness by shelling out to the `sf` CLI, and register it so Salesforce links report a last activity time and a `changed` flag instead of `unchecked`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `internal/probe/salesforce` implements `probe.Prober` with `System()` returning `links.SystemSalesforce`.
- [ ] The prober runs `sf data query --query "<SOQL>" --json` through an injectable command runner (a func type), so tests drive it with no CLI and no live org.
- [ ] `internal/links` recognises `force.com` and `salesforce.com` hosts as `SystemSalesforce` and parses the record id (and object name where present) from Lightning URLs (`/lightning/r/<Object>/<Id>/`) and classic record URLs.
- [ ] The bare-id regexes no longer re-match ids that sit inside a harvested URL.
- [ ] Object typing resolves from the Lightning URL object when present, otherwise from the 3-char id key prefix. All-digit records query `Case` by `CaseNumber`. An unknown prefix renders `unchecked` with a no-probe reason.
- [ ] The prober queries `LastModifiedDate` per record and parses Salesforce's timestamp format.
- [ ] Every record and object name is validated against a strict charset before it enters a SOQL string. Anything that fails renders `unchecked`.
- [ ] The unchecked invariant holds: a missing or unauthenticated CLI, a non-zero `sf` status, or a query error makes every affected Salesforce link `unchecked` (`ReasonAuth` for auth failures, `ReasonError` otherwise), never `changed=false`, and never advances a watermark for an unchecked link.
- [ ] Salesforce moves out of the README's `no probe available` list, and the CLI-auth model is documented (no `salesforce_token` key in the secrets file).
- [ ] Tests cover Case-by-number, object-from-URL, unknown-prefix unchecked, CLI-failure unchecked (auth versus error), the runner argument shape, and the SOQL injection guard.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- The Salesforce prober, its CLI runner seam, and its rate limiter.
- Salesforce URL parsing and the bare-id-inside-URL fix in `internal/links`.
- Registration wiring and the README update.

**Out of scope:**
- Jira and GitHub probers. They stay `no probe available`.
- A direct Salesforce HTTP or OAuth client. This issue uses the `sf` CLI only.

## Technical Details

Follow the shape of the existing probers under `internal/probe/{slack,gmail,aha,gdrive}`. Each is a `Client` with a `New(...)` constructor, functional options, a `rate.Limiter` from `golang.org/x/time/rate`, one decode struct for the fields it reads, and a `Probe` that returns `map[string]probe.Result`. The HTTP probers take an injectable base URL and use `httptest.Server` in tests. The Salesforce prober takes an injectable command runner instead:

```go
type Runner func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) ([]byte, error)
```

The default runner uses `exec.CommandContext` and captures stdout. `sf ... --json` writes a JSON envelope to stdout even on failure, with a non-zero `status` and a `message`, so parse the envelope rather than relying on the exit code alone.

Record sources from `internal/links`:
- Bare Salesforce ids from the `sfid` regex (today it matches only 00-prefixed ids).
- Case numbers from the `Case NNNNN` pattern, with `Record` set to the number.
- New: ids and object names parsed from Salesforce URLs.

Group the links before querying. One SOQL per sObject for id lookups (`SELECT Id, LastModifiedDate FROM <object> WHERE Id IN (...)`), one for Case numbers (`SELECT CaseNumber, LastModifiedDate FROM Case WHERE CaseNumber IN (...)`). Prefix-to-object map for ids without an object hint: 001 Account, 003 Contact, 005 User, 006 Opportunity, 00Q Lead, 500 Case, 701 Campaign, 800 Contract.

Parse `LastModifiedDate` with the layout `2006-01-02T15:04:05.000-0700`.

Registration differs from the HTTP probers. Salesforce auth lives in the `sf` CLI's own store, not the off-store `secrets.json`, so there is no flat `salesforce_token` key. Register the prober when the `sf` binary is available, or behind a config toggle, and set that in `resolveProbeDeps` in `internal/cli/probe.go`. The composition root is `internal/probe/probeset`.

Related: #3 (the probe engine and the four shipped probers).

## Testing

```
nix develop --command go test ./internal/probe/salesforce/ ./internal/links/
nix develop --command golangci-lint run
nix flake check
```

Tests must not invoke the real `sf` CLI or reach a live org. Use the injectable runner with recorded JSON fixtures. No credential value appears in any test or log.

step_history.note: Classified as standard: multi-file (probe/salesforce, internal/links, cli/probe.go, probeset, README) with fully prescriptive acceptance criteria and technical details.
Plan written with 4 tasks: links Salesforce URL parsing + broadened bare-id regex, the sf-CLI prober with injectable runner and SOQL guard, registration wiring on sf availability, README update.
All 4 tasks implemented and committed: links parsing, sf-CLI prober, registration, README. Running full verification.
